prevalence.trend: decreasing due to flour fortification programs

riskFactors: Poor diet low in heme iron
Chronic blood loss from gastrointestinal issues
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Brazilian diagnostic criteria: hemoglobin <12 g/dL for women, <13 g/dL for men, confirmed by low serum ferritin <15 ng/mL

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 65% among diagnosed cases through SUS supplementation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SUS prenatal screening protocol includes hemoglobin testing at first consultation
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Oral iron supplementation (ferrous sulfate 60 mg elemental iron daily) free via SUS, food fortification program with iron in wheat and corn flour
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Parasitic infections (hookworm) in North/Northeast regions, dietary iron deficiency due to reliance on non-fortified foods in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Mandatory flour fortification with iron and folic acid since 2004, universal prenatal iron supplementation via SUS for at-risk women
